title: Hikari — Vulkan Ray Tracer
description: Physically-based path tracer built from scratch in C++ with Vulkan. Implements Monte Carlo light transport, importance sampling, emissive materials, and a denoiser. Accumulation buffers allow progressive rendering convergence — the kind of system where correctness is verifiable by math and every bug is a physics violation.

title: Majin — Game Engine
description: 3D engine in C++23 with Vulkan. Swap chain management, RAII device lifetime, command buffer architecture, and a 2D physics simulation. Written to understand how rendering infrastructure actually works, not to wrap someone else's abstraction.

title: Chess Engine
description: Negamax search with alpha-beta pruning, transposition tables, and PeSTO evaluation — all within Sebastian Lague's 1024-token challenge constraint. Hard token limit forces every decision to earn its space, which turns out to be a good way to learn what actually matters in a search algorithm.

title: FEP University Chatbot
description: Production AI chatbot deployed on-premises for a Portuguese university. TypeScript + Python stack, Azure OpenAI for LLM capacity, custom deployment pipeline. Real users, real queries, real failure modes — the kind of project where 'it works on my machine' is not an acceptable answer.

title: DeepMine
description: Strategic 2D mining game built in 72 hours for Ludum Dare 57. Grid-based terrain with physics-driven chain-reaction collapses — each dig decision changes the structural integrity of the board. Shipped solo in a weekend jam.

title: Turbo Soccer
description: Local-multiplayer car-soccer in Unity. FEUP-themed arena with obstacle navigation, power-ups, and split-screen play. Built as a team project — the kind of thing that teaches you more about scoping than any course.
